refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system modules.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated high-reliability network link with QoS prioritization.
DP3: Integrate real-time packet loss monitoring and dynamic adjustment algorithms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and network link quality.
Dependency on real-time monitoring data for adaptation.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates communication quality management within the protocol layer, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
Assumes availability of sufficient processing resources for adaptive protocol.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding latency constraints explicitly in FR to balance packet loss and responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation communication management, including protocol implementation and packet loss control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to physical and logical network infrastructure for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send communication quality metrics and status to NGHE Solution and other monitoring modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and task instructions from NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: Contains overall system coordination but not specialized communication protocol logic.
Vehicle module focuses on physical actuation, minimal containment of communication protocol.
Primary containment for communication protocol and packet loss management.
Safety services monitor alerts but do not manage communication quality.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP to Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, reducing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with NGHE Solution for telemetry reporting.
Risk of interface complexity if Teleoperation module must interact extensively with Safety Services for alerts.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify interface responsibilities between Teleoperation and Safety Services to avoid diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR by implementing adaptive communication protocols to control packet loss.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry data reporting may require coordination with NGHE Solution, risking diffusion.
Interface complexity with Safety Services needs monitoring.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment scores and low diffusion, appropriate for DP implementation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Telemetry interface and validating against customer limits.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of communication quality management to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add explicit latency requirements to FR to balance packet loss and responsiveness.
Define interface protocols between Teleoperation and Safety Services to minimize diffusion.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignment meets the requirements with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. Verification and containment are feasible with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system modules.

revise.why: To incorporate latency constraints explicitly for balanced teleoperation performance.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Processing resources are sufficient for adaptive protocol execution.
evidence_needed: Performance testing data on packet loss and latency under representative network conditions.
Interface definition documents between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
